name: Shrimp Custard
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Fish sauce (NAM PLA or NUOC MAM, or soy sauce)
recipeInstructions:
- NAM PLA or NUOC MAM, or soy sauce.
ÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿÿ ÿÿÿÿÿÿ
1. In each of 4 custard cups or bowls (3/4 cup size), place 1/4 of the shrimp. Set cups in a large baking pan at least 2 inches deep.
2. In a bowl, combine milk, eggs, sherry, 2 teaspoons fish sauce, ginger, garlic, oil, and pepper; beat lightly just to blend. Pour 1/4 of the mixture into each cup.
3. Set pan on center rack of an oven set to 325°F. Pour boiling water around cups in the pan until it reaches the level of the custard. Bake until custard jiggles only slightly when gently shaken, 25-35 minutes. Lift cups from the pan and let stand at least 10 minutes. If made ahead, let cool, then cover and chill up to a day.
4. Meanwhile, toast sesame seeds in a frying pan over medium-low heat (6-8 inches), shaking the pan often until seeds are golden, about 8 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside. Just before serving, garnish custards with sesame seed. Offer warm or cold, adding fish sauce to taste.
